title: Tukimihia Simpkins

infobox.value: [Prop](/wiki/Rugby_league_positions#prop_forward "Rugby league positions"), [Lock](/wiki/Rugby_league_positions#Loose_forward_/_Lock_forward "Rugby league positions"), [Second-row](/wiki/Rugby_league_positions#Second-row_forward "Rugby league positions")

(born 24 December 2001) is a professional [rugby league](/wiki/Rugby_league "Rugby league") footballer who plays as a [prop](/wiki/Rugby_league_positions#prop_forward "Rugby league positions") for the [Gold Coast Titans](/wiki/Gold_Coast_Titans "Gold Coast Titans") in the [National Rugby League](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League") (NRL).

["NRL: Top Kiwi prospect Tuki Simpkins joins Wests Tigers after player switch"](https://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/123878200/nrl-top-kiwi-prospect-tuki-simpkins-joins-wests-tigers-after-player-switch). 5 January 2021.

["All Black, white and gold: How New Zealand rugby let Wests Tigers' giant escape"](https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/all-black-white-and-gold-how-new-zealand-rugby-let-wests-tigers-giant-escape-20210717-p58ajv.html). 17 July 2021.
["Cowboys, Wests Tigers announce player swap | NRL News"](https://www.zerotackle.com/cowboys-wests-tigers-announce-player-swap-kane-bradley-tukimihia-simpkins-90854/). 4 January 2021.
["Magpies fall to Bears in wet and gritty clash"](https://www.weststigers.com.au/news/2021/03/21/magpies-fall-to-bears-in-wet-and-gritty-clash/). *Wests Tigers*. 21 March 2021.
["Emerging forward to join Titans after grand final berth"](https://www.titans.com.au/news/2024/09/19/emerging-forward-to-join-titans-after-grand-final-berth/). *www.titans.com.au*.

["Wests Tigers star Tuki Simpkins under investigation by police"](https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/wests-tigers-star-tuki-simpkins-under-investigation-by-police/news-story/b5be49612d75328dc0434e2bc44aefb7?amp/). *www.news.com.au*.

NZ rugby league footballer

Tukimihia Simpkins

| Weight | 108 kg (17 st 0 lb) |

| | Club | | | | | | | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Years | Team | Pld | T | G | FG | P | | 2021–22 | [Wests Tigers](/wiki/Wests_Tigers "Wests Tigers") | 5 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | | 2025– | [Gold Coast Titans](/wiki/Gold_Coast_Titans "Gold Coast Titans") | 2 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | |  | Total | 7 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | | Representative | | | | | | | | Years | Team | Pld | T | G | FG | P | | 2023 | [Māori All Stars](/wiki/New_Zealand_M%C4%81ori_rugby_league_team "New Zealand Māori rugby league team") | 1 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | | |

**Tukimihia Simpkins** (born 24 December 2001) is a professional [rugby league](/wiki/Rugby_league "Rugby league") footballer who plays as a [prop](/wiki/Rugby_league_positions#prop_forward "Rugby league positions") for the [Gold Coast Titans](/wiki/Gold_Coast_Titans "Gold Coast Titans") in the [National Rugby League](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League") (NRL).

## Background

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tukimihia_Simpkins&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Background")]

Simpkins was born in [Rotorua](/wiki/Rotorua "Rotorua"), New Zealand, and is of NZ [Māori](/wiki/M%C4%81ori_people "Māori people") descent.

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tukimihia_Simpkins&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

### Early career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tukimihia_Simpkins&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Early career")]

Simpkins begun his career playing rugby union for Rotorua Boys High School first XV. He signed with [Super Rugby](/wiki/Super_Rugby "Super Rugby") franchise the [Chiefs](/wiki/Waikato_Chiefs "Waikato Chiefs") when he was 15 and held brief talks with the [Crusaders](/wiki/Canterbury_Crusaders "Canterbury Crusaders"). He was first spotted by [North Queensland Cowboys](/wiki/North_Queensland_Cowboys "North Queensland Cowboys") scouts at the New Zealand 15s rugby league tournament in Rotorua in 2016.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

He became a New Zealand Secondary Schools rugby league representative and played for the New Zealand Residents team in 2019 before heading to Australia in 2020.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

In 2020, he was signed by the North Queensland Cowboys, he played one game with North Queensland feeder club the [Townsville Blackhawks](/wiki/Townsville_Blackhawks "Townsville Blackhawks") in the under 20s competition. He was brought to the [Wests Tigers](/wiki/Wests_Tigers "Wests Tigers") on a three-year deal in a player swap deal with [Kane Bradley](/wiki/Kane_Bradley "Kane Bradley").[[5]](#cite_note-5)

### 2021

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tukimihia_Simpkins&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: 2021")]

Simpkins began the 2021 season playing for [Western Suburbs](/wiki/Western_Suburbs_Magpies "Western Suburbs Magpies") in the [New South Wales Cup](/wiki/New_South_Wales_Cup "New South Wales Cup").[[6]](#cite_note-6)

Simpkins made his debut from the bench in round 18 of the [2021 NRL season](/wiki/2021_NRL_season "2021 NRL season") for Wests Tigers in their 42–24 victory over the [Brisbane Broncos](/wiki/Brisbane_Broncos "Brisbane Broncos") at [Lang Park](/wiki/Lang_Park "Lang Park").[[7]](#cite_note-7)

### 2024

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tukimihia_Simpkins&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: 2024")]

Simpkins won the Queensland Cup starting at prop in the [Norths Devils](/wiki/Norths_Devils "Norths Devils") 34-20 grand final win over the [Redcliffe Dolphins](/wiki/Redcliffe_Dolphins "Redcliffe Dolphins").[[8]](#cite_note-8)
On 20 September, Simpkins signed a contract to join strugglers the [Gold Coast](/wiki/Gold_Coast_Titans "Gold Coast Titans") ahead of the [2025 NRL season](/wiki/2025_NRL_season "2025 NRL season").[[9]](#cite_note-9)

### 2025

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tukimihia_Simpkins&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: 2025")]

Tuki debuted for the [Gold Coast Titans](/wiki/Gold_Coast_Titans "Gold Coast Titans") from the bench in there 28-8 round 15 win against [Manly Sea Eagles](/wiki/Manly_Sea_Eagles "Manly Sea Eagles") , it was his first NRL game in 2 years.

## Controversy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tukimihia_Simpkins&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Controversy")]

On 24 January 2022, it was revealed that Simpkins had allegedly been involved in a car accident in which he mounted the kerb and drove through a fence while also being unlicensed. Simpkins then allegedly fled the scene of the accident but later handed himself into NSW Police. The Wests Tigers club later released a statement saying "Wests Tigers have been made aware of an incident involving NRL player Tukimihia Simpkins, the club has alerted the NRL to the matter and will continue to work with the NRL Integrity Unit as needed".[[10]](#cite_note-10)
